text_source: So, ready to try again?
Yes...
All right, let's figure out what we learned.\n
The bitterness of the sap intensifies after it\n
hardens...
So we haven't been giving it the time to harden\n
by using sap taken directly from the tree\n
itself.
The bitterness still persists even then, so\n
NOW we'll try absorbing it with amam powder.
That sounds right...
Rulutieh gives a small nod, then begins to mix\n
amam powder from a small jar into a bowl of\n
sap.
Let me know if you want me to jump in. We can\n
take turns at it.
Thank you, but... I should... be fine.
I'd better let her focus. I'm a complete\n
amateur at this, after all.
Now, we should leave it to set, and then\n
separate it after all the amam has sunk to\n
the bottom.
Right...
That way, all the bitterness will be\n
concentrated in the powder, leaving us with\n
what we want.
We originally tried to mix in ash to do this,\n
but who'd have guessed amam powder would do\n
the same job?
Just as expected, the powder sinks to the\n
bottom, leaving a clear liquid on top...
...Oh, it's faintly sweet now!
Rulutieh smiles as she tastes the final result.
That powerful flavor it had is all but... gone.\n
It's strange, tasting it like this...
We can usually get this far with no problems.\n
But now...
Next, we need to boil it since it's so thin,\n
but this is the tricky part.
Rulutieh, would you do the honors?
Y-Yes...
Rulutieh separates and scoops up our prize into\n
a pot, moving it over to the cookfire.
There... It's ready.
All right, I'm ready too. Let's try not to burn\n
it this time.
And don't worry about messing up, OK?\n
Failure is always an option. Just relax.
U-Understood...
Rulutieh stirs the supernatant liquid in the\n
pot with an anxious expression on her face...
And finally, the clear liquid begins to turn\n
amber!
Now, hold up--maybe we should take the pot off\n
of the stove for a m--
Oh...
Almost instantly, the thick amber mixture\n
blackens and begins to give off a smoky odor.
It's all burnt...
We failed again. I doubt any of this is edible,\n
either...
I'm sorry... I-I... I failed...
Don't apologize! I could tell you were paying\n
the utmost attention. You did well.
Who'd have thought this stuff burns so easily,\n
though? Boiling it is turning out to be a\n
pain...
We, could, um... try heating it through a\n
water bath, maybe...?
No, that'll take way too much time to hit its\n
boiling point. It wouldn't be efficient at all.
Yes... y-you're right, of course.
We've come this far, though. It feels like a\n
waste to give up here...
If only I were... better. A-At this, I mean.
This is the first time you've done this, right?\n
Nobody's expecting you to have it down right\n
away.
Every failure's a learning experience,\n
Rulutieh. We'll get there eventually!
I... suppose you're right, yes.
Well, being all depressed about it isn't going\n
to do us any good. Let's move on for now, huh?
Yes, of course!
Rulutieh takes the remaining murky fluid and\n
pours it into another pot on the fire...
After a while, the liquid in the pot turns\n
viscous without burning at all.
And for some reason, this one doesn't burn at\n
all... I don't get it. What's different?
We've figured out a recipe, at least. Cooling\n
it in a mold makes for a sweet, gelatinous\n
snack.
Not quite the result we wanted, but tasty all\n
the same.
Looks like it's just about done.\n
What do you think?
Y-Yes, I think so too...
Rulutieh takes the mold out of the water and\n
flips it over onto a cutting board...
Then, she takes a knife and deftly cuts the\n
jiggling mass into neat, orderly cubes.
And that should do it!
Now, to sample our work.
Hromf... *Smack... smack*...
Ahh, delicious!
Yes, it is! Such a... bizarre texture.\n
It's strange, but... very good...
Hard to believe something this good came out\n
of our random experiments, huh?
Making this stuff has definitely been our\n
focus lately.
Hee hee...
Hm?
You're amazing, Sir Haku. You came up with\n
something so fantastic, completely by accident!
It was more coincidence than anything. Besides,\n
I had you here to help me, didn't I?
If it wasn't for you, this whole plan would\n
have been dead in the water.
Oh, that smells so nice! You made that jiggly\n
stuff again didn't you?
It gives off a very pleasing scent, I must\n
admit.
Uh-oh, looks like we've attracted the pack.\n
Hide the goods before the hounds steal our\n
share, Rulutieh!
Too bad, but this wolf's already got her prize!
Mmm! Delicious.
This sweetness and strange, alluring mouthfeel...\n
I doubt I'll ever grow tired of this, truly.
As Nekone ruminates, Kuon spears another piece\n
on a toothpick and places it in her waiting\n
mouth.
Here you go, Nekone.
Ah, d-dear sister--
Mmff...
Nekone looks embarrassed at being hand-fed, but\n
her expression turns to a smile in short order.
Those two sure do get along. It puts a smile\n
on my face to see them enjoying themselves...
Hromf... *munch*... Ah, delicious!\n
Haku, love, do you mind if I have a bite\n
of this?
...Typically you ask that BEFORE you eat it,\n
Atuy.
Hey, what's this one? Looks like you two have\n
been working on something else, eh?
Atuy bends over the pot containing our failed\n
attempt, scooping some of it up on a spatula.
Wait, that's--
I think I'll just have a taste of this.
Before I can tell her it was an unsuccessful\n
batch, she slurps it up.
Haku, love, what's this black syrup you've\n
got here? It's so sweet!
...Huh?
Oh, now THERE'S an idea. Hold on.
Atuy drizzles the black syrup over a piece of\n
gelatin and pops it into her mouth...
Oh, love, how DARE you? Keeping this syrup a\n
secret from us! It's wonderful!
Really? Let's see...
...Hey, this IS good!
This texture and sweet scent... Subtle notes\n
of blended flavor... Truly, appearances can\n
deceive.
They're eating the burnt batch like there's no\n
tomorrow. Is it really that good...?
*Munch... munch*...
Huh? I... uh... thought... T-Taste testing is\n
an important step of... the process, isn't it?
Here now, love. Open wide.
She stabs one of the snacks with a toothpick,\n
then dips it in syrup, holding it out to me\n
expectantly.
Why does everybody try to feed me like this?\n
*Sigh*...
Reluctantly, I eat the substance right off the\n
end of the toothpick.
Hey, this is...
The snack is only faintly sweet without anything\n
on it, but with the syrup, it's VERY sweet!
The black syrup's flavor is a little strong on\n
its own, but as a sauce, it balances out well.
Never would've noticed on my own.\n
I was so wrapped up in trying to perfect it,\n
I never thought...
I was sure this was a failure, but it turns out\n
there's a perfect use for it!
I-I see what you mean, now...\n
"Failure is always an option."
W-Well, when I said that, I meant--\n
It's... a little different in this case--
So, Haku, what're you gonna call this stuff?
What it's called? Uh. Tree... sap... snack?
No, I mean a proper name for it.
I've never seen anything like it, and it's\n
REALLY good. It ought to have a name!
Come on, do we have to NAME it? It just came\n
about by coincidence; it's not THAT big a\n
deal...
Points for trying, but I think you're wrong,\n
love. I've never tasted anything like this!
Kuon's right. This deserves a right proper name.
I agree. It would be inconvenient not to be\n
able to refer to this concoction by a name.
I guess that's a fair point...
U-Um...
I th-think these snacks would be very happy\n
if you named them, Sir Haku.
I, um--s-sorry, that... sounds silly when\n
I say it out loud...
No, you're right. It would be more convenient\n
to have a name for this thing.
It's going to get confusing if we keep calling\n
it "the sweet stuff Haku and Rulutieh make\n
sometimes."
A name, huh? I've never been good at that kind\n
of stuff. Let's see... uh...
Well, it's your recipe, isn't it? Why don't\n
you just name it after yourself?
What, "Haku?" Shall we call it that, then?
What kind of crazy logic is THAT? You can't\n
just name it after a person!
I dunno. It's pretty common for foods to be\n
named after people.
Whoa, hang on, hang on.
I'm not sure I'm comfortable with the idea of\n
it being JUST my name and nothing else.
I make eye contact with the girl standing next\n
to me, and I get an idea...
The person who actually MADE all this is\n
Rulutieh, so why don't we base it on her name\n
instead?
Something like... Rulu, for example.
After that, I got too lazy to make the stuff\n
on my own, so I shared the recipe with a\n
nearby teahouse.
They began to offer it, and soon enough, it\n
became known as the capital's hallmark\n
confection!
Every time we pass a shop with a "Rulu" sign\n
out front, Rulutieh can't help but get\n
embarrassed...
But I don't think it's my imagination that she\n
likes seeing others enjoy what we made together.
...and Kuon seems to be in a strangely good\n
mood since all of this happened.
I just hope she's not planning anything weird\n
again...
I keep pace with Nekone as she turns down a\n
busy street, leading me along.
Oshtor asked me to accompany her today, but\n
something feels off...
Nekone's been awfully quiet, for one thing.
It's not as though she's a lively person, but\n
she's never as quiet as THIS...
Is she just in a bad mood, or is it something\n
else?
Is it because we're going to the other\n
Imperial Guard's manor? Oshtor's counterpart?
Oshtor gave us a letter to deliver to the guy\n
on his behalf... I think his name is Mikazuchi?
I only caught a glimpse of him before, but he\n
seems like the violent type. Maybe...?
Don't tell me you're nervous, Nekone?
...No. I am not nervous.
Yeah? If you say so, I guess.
All we really have to do is drop off the letter.\n
Not sure why that has her in such a mood...
Or maybe...\n
Nah, couldn't be. Could it?
Speak.
Is it that time of the month? I mean, n-not\n
that it's--you're probably way too young for--
*WHUMP*
BWUH!?
Before I can even finish my thought, Nekone\n
savagely kicks me across the shins.
Hnngh, ow--Wh-Why would you do that?
I admit I do not know what you were accusing\n
me of, but a judicious response felt\n
appropriate.
Ow, shit. Are all kids so mean at this age?
The rest of the journey toward Mikazuchi's\n
manor is silent...
We inform the guards we're here on business\n
from Oshtor, and they direct us to the\n
training grounds.
The manor feels similar to Oshtor's, but it\n
feels... more austere. More military?
Lord Mikazuchi prizes the arts of war over\n
all else, as I understand it.
The arts of war?
A sharp cry from nearby interrupts us.
Form one, begin!
Guards
Sir!
On the grounds ahead, assembled soldiers\n
practice battlefield techniques in perfect\n
unison.
Form two!
Wow, impressive...
Sure, Oshtor's subordinates are organized--\n
but I've never seen discipline this rigid.\n
Amazing...
As I look on, dumbstruck, the man giving orders\n
notices us and raises his hand to the soldiers.
Atten-SHUN!
*Snap*
Despite their arduous exercise, not one of the\n
soldiers looks even winded as they snap to\n
attention.
The man shouting commands leaves them standing\n
like that as he crosses the grounds to us.
Hm...
As he grows closer, I recognize his face, of\n
course.
The Imperial Guard of the Left, Mikazuchi.
He grins menacingly when he spots Nekone\n
beside me.
Ah, Nekone. I recall seeing you recently, but\n
it's been a while since we've had a chance to\n
talk.
Uh oh.
His smile is almost sinister, like a predator\n
that's just identified the perfect prey.
Now that I meet him in person, he has an even\n
scarier presence to him than a Boro-Gigiri.
So this is the other half of the Mikado's\n
Twin Shields...
You look well, girl.
But you're still so small. Eat more, eh?\n
It will make you bigger in time.
The words are those of a caring elder, but his\n
tone makes it sound like he's telling livestock\n
to fatten up...
...Nekone, why are you hiding behind me?
Nnnngh...
I didn't even notice Nekone moving to cower\n
behind my legs.
Then, as I scoot over to let her and Mikazuchi\n
speak face-to-face--
*Fwip*
*Fwoosh*
Every time I reposition myself, Nekone zips\n
behind me in the blink of an eye.
What the hell is she do--\n
Oohh. I get it.
Now, I suddenly understand the reason for\n
Nekone's discomfort on the way here.
She's even more scared of Mikazuchi than I am!\n
Not like I can blame her...
P-Please--accept this missive from Lord Oshtor,\n
s-sir.
Struggling to keep the tremble out of her\n
voice, Nekone holds out the envelope from\n
behind me.
Hey, now, Nekone. Don't you think you're being\n
a little rude?
Her attitude doesn't change, of course.
Hn.
Mikazuchi's eyes narrow, possibly in offense\n
at Nekone's rudeness.
Eep!
God, this guy is scary as hell.
I can feel Nekone shivering in fear behind me.\n
If looks could kill, Mikazuchi would be an\n
assassin.
I... I'm not afraid of you!
I-I-I am not afraid of you at all!
*Swish* *jab*
As Nekone proclaims her defiance, she throws\n
anemic little punches as if to prove her words.
Of course, she's doing all this while still\n
hiding behind me...
W--Would you stop using me as a shield!?
*Mumble, mumble*...
At Nekone's declaration, the previously silent\n
soldiers begin to grow visibly uneasy.
Guard
Is that girl mad? Challenging the general\n
himself...
He'll utterly destroy her!
Th-This guy's so bloodthirsty he inspires THAT\n
much fear in his own soldiers? What in the\n
world?
*Swish* *swoosh*
Nekone doesn't stop swinging, still throwing\n
little punches around my legs.
Oho...?
Mikazuchi grins wolfishly, eyes gleaming as\n
though he's deciding how best to devour poor\n
Nekone.
A-A-Are you looking for a fight?\n
Well, you'll have to get past him f-first.
Wait, WHAT? D-Don't pull me into this any more\n
than you already have!
Stop using me as a shield and--\n
Damn it, she won't budge.
Somehow, I manage to turn myself to push her\n
forward, but she won't move--in fact, SHE'S\n
pushing ME.
Hey--hey, stop that!
Th-That man... he intends on championing that\n
young lady's challenge to Lord Mikazuchi...
The idiot. Is he throwing his life away for\n
nothing?
I commend the noble fool for his courage.\n
I'll see to it he receives a proper burial...
Are you all idiots!? How the hell is this\n
CHALLENGING him? I'm going to get killed at\n
this rate!
*Shove, shove--*
Would you PLEASE stop push--
Nekone gives a rough shove, and suddenly, my\n
face is bare inches from Mikazuchi's.
Mnnhh. Heh. Aheh heh heh.
Mikazuchi's lips curl back into a smile, and he\n
jerks his head to indicate we should follow\n
him.
Wh-What?
...We're following him. Go.
Huh? H-Hey! I got it, OK? You don't have to\n
keep PUSHING--Damn it, Nekone, stop--
...Somehow, we find ourselves invited into\n
Mikazuchi's manor proper.
...The air in here is thick and awkward...
Neither Mikazuchi nor Nekone has said a word\n
since we came inside. They only glare at each\n
other.
Nekone, however, is beginning to crack under\n
the pressure--and she breaks eye contact,\n
glancing down.
*Shove, shove*...
Could she stop trying to shield herself with\n
me? We're sitting in CHAIRS, for God's sake.
Miruhj, tea.
By your will, my lord.
A smooth, erudite-sounding voice precedes what\n
must be Mikazuchi's squire entering the room.
Your tea, my lord.
The boy Mikazuchi called Miruhj efficiently\n
arranges the cups...
Please enjoy. If you'll excuse me, my lords and\n
lady.
It's all right, you can stay. In fact, could\n
you, uh, do something about the atmosphere in\n
here?
Nnnhh...
Nekone prods me with the envelope Oshtor gave\n
us.
Huh? You want me to give this to him?
Nekone nods vigorously. The poor girl is so\n
scared, tears are welling up in her eyes.
Fine, fine... Here.
I grab the envelope from Nekone and proffer it\n
to Mikazuchi.
Lord Mikazuchi, Oshtor himself entrusted us\n
with this missive, if you'd be so kind as to\n
receive it.
Is that so?
Mikazuchi takes the message and sets it aside.\n
He doesn't seem too concerned with\n
formalities...
...You're not going to read it?
Is that... an ORDER you're giving me, boy?
Mikazuchi's eyes snap wide as he glares at me.\n
Yikes.
I don't get this guy's thinking at all. How the\n
hell would he arrive at that conclusion?
My dear brother requires an answer of you\n
before we can return to him, sir.
R-Read the letter and furnish us with your\n
response!
What, NOW you grow a spine? Get out from\n
behind me and say that stuff to him directly,\n
damn it!
Mikazuchi seems to have heard her perfectly,\n
for he unfolds the envelope and begins to\n
read...
After looking through Oshtor's message,\n
Mikazuchi looks at us with that same wolfish\n
grin.
*Shove, shove, shove*...
For God's sake, Nekone, will you cut that ou--
So. You're Oshtor's man... This letter describes\n
you as quite the character.
I-I see...
Wait, why is Oshtor outing me as his agent?\n
I thought he wanted to keep that a secret...
Very interesting. Interesting indeed...
Huh?
Oshtor's respect is not won so easily.\n
You've piqued my curiosity, boy.
I look forward to whatever entertainment\n
you'll bring me.
Wait, entertainment...?
You WILL entertain me, will you not, boy?
Wait, wait, wait, wait. I don't have anything\n
up my sleeve. What exactly is he expecting\n
from me?
Well, then. It appears as though Lord\n
Mikazuchi's business is with you alone, so\n
I'll take my leave.
You wait just a goddamn second, Nekone!\n
You're not going anywhere--
Unhand me! H-How dare you touch me there,\n
cur!?
Yeah, that protest only holds water if you\n
actually have curves. Sorry, kid.
Grrr...
Hnnngh...
Nekone and I lock like grappling bulls, trying\n
to push each other toward Mikazuchi...
...Heh. To think little Nekone has opened up to\n
another besides her "dear brother."\n
Monumental, that.
Open u--I-I beg your pardon, sir!
Blushing, Nekone? Ah, I've gone and entranced\n
another one... It's hard being so handsome\n
sometimes.
Unfortunately, I can't recommend falling in\n
love with me. You'll get burned if you get too\n
close.
But maybe you should wait until there's a\n
visible difference between your front side\n
and back--
HYAH!
Hnngaaah!!
Nekone deals the fateful blow and storms out\n
of the room, her face utterly red.
W-Wait... you... Oh, God, my precious parts--\n
Th-That was all your strength...
I try to chase after Nekone, but all I can\n
manage right now is a loping stumble.
Your name is Haku, is it not?
Hnngh?
Mikazuchi is glaring sharper daggers at me\n
than ever before.
Shit. That little display just now probably\n
annoyed him...
I swallow dryly, dreading whatever Mikazuchi\n
has in store for me next. He continues\n
speaking.
It seems as though that girl has placed her\n
trust in you.
Are--are you fucking serious? After what just\n
happened? I thought my jewels were done for!
Mikazuchi glances down, speaking almost to\n
I used to have a sister myself, you know.\n
Our relationship wasn't especially bad.
Inform Oshtor that all will be arranged as he\n
wills it. And... I trust you to protect that\n
girl, understand?
What's that supposed to...
With that, Mikazuchi falls silent. Again.\n
I think... I'm starting to understand this guy\n
a little more.
He really is concerned for Nekone, huh?
I will see our guests out, Lord Mikazuchi.
The squire from before reappears, sensing\n
that Mikazuchi is done talking for now.
If you would follow me, please?
Yeah, all right.
And please, take a token of our hospitality\n
back with you. I'll show you to the main\n
entrance...
This boy seems so kind and gentle.\n
Totally the opposite of his commander...
By the time I take Miruhj's gift and exit the\n
manor, it dawns on me just how late it is.
God, I finally got out of there...
All the tension leaves me in one go, and\n
suddenly, I feel exhausted.
A job well done, Sir Haku.
Nekone comes running over as I exit.
Nekone? Were you waiting out here that whole\n
time?
I actually AM capable of worrying about you,\n
you know.
I don't believe it. Of all people, Nekone was\n
worried about me?
I was far from certain of whether you would\n
come out of there alive, after all.
H-Ha ha...
Her words may sound like a joke, but I'm\n
getting chills just thinking about the\n
possibility.
Shall we make our return? There is no reason\n
to tarry here any longer.
Sounds good. I'll fill you in on the details\n
on the way back.
I take a bite of my skewer as we walk,\n
savoring the taste.
Mmf. Looks like I made the right choice.\n
That stall's kokoromo is pretty tasty.
The juicy fat mixes perfectly with sweet and\n
spicy sauce, suffusing my mouth with bold,\n
rich flavor...
Mysterious duo right
Mysterious duo left
And what exactly do you two want?
All of a sudden, that mysterious duo from\n
before appears on either side of me, quiet\n
and unannounced.
You two just disappeared as soon as we arrived\n
at the capital. Where did you get off to,\n
anyway?
Yeah, you're about as chatty as I remember.\n
Good talk.
I can't even make out their expressions because\n
of those head coverings...
Y'know, it's kinda hard to see where I'm going\n
with you two all snuggled up to me like this--
These two don't seem to have any concept of\n
personal space, but at least they're not\n
hurting anyone.
I don't THINK they have any particular ill\n
intent toward me, at least...
...What?
I can't see their eyes, so it's just a feeling,\n
but I could swear they're staring at me. Or...
Or they're staring at the bag of skewers I just\n
bought. Of course.
Oh. You guys want some?
You know, I'm not sure why I was expecting a\n
response.
Here, dig in.
I hand them each a skewer.
They both accept the skewers, then quietly tuck\n
in, holding them up beneath their masks...
I mean, I think they're eating? Hard to see\n
anything through all that cloth.
You like it?
In unison, they nod their approval.
Yeah? Glad you like it.
We walk in silence for a while, eating while\n
we take in the city... It's kind of nice,\n
actually.
Ah, Sir Haku...?
Oh, hullo, love. Out shopping?
Eating out more than shopping, really.\n
What're you two up to?
Rulie tipped me off to the CUTEST hair pins\n
at this one stall. She took me to see the\n
place!
Y-Yes, I think they suit Miss Atuy very well.
Hair pins, huh?
Nearby, I spy the vendor in question, their\n
stall loaded up with hair ornaments of all\n
kinds.
That Rulutieh is interested is a given, but\n
Atuy? I didn't think she'd be into this frilly\n
stuff...
Just taking in the sights on your own, then,\n
love?
On my own? What are you talking about,\n
I've got th... Huh?
That strange pair, of course, has disappeared\n
without so much as a word. Figures.
This again...?
Those two just come and go as they please,\n
huh.
"Again?"
A-At any rate, I was just taking a walk and\n
got hungry. I picked up some food along the\n
way.
Oh! That smells good.
Atuy's ears prick up as she takes notice of\n
the skewers.
Hee. I'll just have a little sample, here--
Atuy snatches the final skewer out of the bag\n
before I can protest.
Wh--Hey? That was the last one!
*Hromf, munch*...
I-I already took a bite out of that one, too...
M-Miss Atuy?
Mmf, thif if really good--ulp.\n
Hey, could I have another?
Like hell! That was the last one.
Aw, that's too bad. Sorry, love.
Why don't we go and get some more, then, eh?\n
Come on, it was this way, right?
Atuy wraps her arm around mine before I can\n
protest.
C'mon, Rulie, let's go.
Um...
And so, I ended up going back the way I came\n
with Rulutieh and Atuy in tow.
So did you already buy that hair pin you were\n
looking for?
Well, there were so many kinds, I couldn't make\n
up my mind! Shopping in the capital is\n
mind-blowing.
Um... I-I thought that red one looked very\n
nice on you.
Hee! Rulie, you know how to flatter a girl.
What did it look like?
Well, it was about this big... and had a\n
floral pattern all over.
Yeah, that one was really pretty...
Oh... I'm sorry, I didn't mean--
Aw, no need to apologize. It's nobody's fault\n
but mine, dear.
Judging by their disappointment, it sounds like\n
someone else bought it while they were\n
browsing.
And besides, it's more romantic to get that\n
sort of thing from your lover as a gift, don't\n
you think?
He'd say something like, "Atuy, this is the\n
only charm I could find that matches your\n
beauty..."
And then he'll gently stroke my hair, and slide\n
the pin on while staring into my eyes, and\n
then--
Yeah, yeah. Keep the fantasies to a minimum\n
around me, please.
It doesn't even have to be that expensive, as\n
long as it's chosen by my sweetheart's own\n
hand.
Atuy gives a small, embarrassed laugh.
Oh, but I've forgotten all about the skewers!
Let's hurry up, eh? Wouldn't want those to\n
sell out before I get there, either!
She trots forward, pulling me along by the arm,\n
and Rulutieh struggles to keep up.
Must be tough hanging out with Atuy. Does she\n
pull you around everywhere like she does with\n
me?
N-Not at all! I enjoy every moment...
And besides... those skewers looked, um, very\n
good.
She seems shy about expressing an interest in\n
the food for some reason.
Well, I can guarantee the taste will be up to\n
scratch, if nothing else.
Ah, here we are. 'Scuse me, sir, could we get\n
another bag of these?
Hee. And you're paying, of course--\n
right, love?
She looks eagerly back toward me.
Oh... Um... no, I can...
Urk. Yeah, all right.
I decide to play the gentleman and treat the\n
two of them, pulling out my wallet.
Yawn...
Hm?
Nngh. It's too early in the morning for this\n
much racket...
Worker
Pardon me! Excuse me, sir, coming through--
Whup.
The inn's housekeepers and workers dash up and\n
down the hallway with determination...
Are they setting up for a banquet or something?\n
They're working awfully hard, and awfully\n
early...
Something wrong, Haku?
Kuon nudges me from behind, looking as puzzled\n
as I am.
Oh, morning, Kuon...
Go wash up and get yourself properly awake,\n
huh? Big day today.
Big day?
My confusion only grows. Am I forgetting\n
something obvious?
Uh, were we supposed to do something today?
Haku...
Kuon sounds exasperated.
The nativity festival? Today? We've talked\n
about this before. Multiple times, actually.
You can't have missed all the decorations that\n
have been going up recently...
Nativity... festival?
...Oh. The princess of Yamato's birthday?
I know you're not from around here, but try to\n
show at least a LITTLE interest in local\n
culture.
I do show interest in local culture!\n
Like... the food. And the booze.
A-And the princess's birthday above all, of\n
course. What is she like, anyway?
I mean, I hear a lot about the Mikado, but it\n
doesn't seem like anyone ever talks about his\n
kid.
Well, let's see...
Kuon tilts her head in thought as she conjures\n
a response.
If the rumors are true, she's quite young, but\n
she's supposed to be very intelligent and\n
charming.
...I see.
"Intelligent and charming" is pretty bland,\n
honestly. That just makes her more of an\n
enigma...
Kuon lets out a small sound as she looks at me,\n
smiling.
Could it be a mysterious imperial flower's\n
caught your eye, Haku?
Eh, not particularly. I'm curious, more than\n
anything else.
No point in chasing after the empire's\n
"beautiful flower" if it grows up beyond\n
the clouds.
Honestly, I'm more excited about the vendors\n
at this festival. I bet there'll be loads of\n
food.
Ahaha! Haku, you can really be childlike\n
sometimes.
What, I'm the childlike one? Then why did your\n
tail start wagging when I mentioned the food\n
carts?
Kuon quickly stills her tail with her hands,\n
going red.
Urk.
I-In any case, I was going to ask--Would you\n
want to... go see the festival together?
Huh?
The talk is that the princess will be paraded\n
through the city atop a mikoshi--a grand,\n
mobile shrine.
Kuon grabs my hand and starts pulling me along,\n
innocent wonder on her face at the prospect.
If she wants to spend a day having fun,\n
I can hardly object.
All right, all right. Let me go get dressed,\n
OK? I can't exactly go out looking like this.
OK, but hurry!
Yeah, yeah.
As I turn to go get ready, it suddenly occurs\n
to me...
Where did Rulutieh and Atuy get off to?
Well, they're princesses too, aren't they?\n
They'll have duties of some kind at the palace.
Oh, right.
It's easy to forget they're here as their\n
fathers' representatives. I'm so familiar with\n
them, now...
Nekone and Kiwru will be with them, too.
Shame we won't all be able to see it together,\n
but it's a festival, isn't it? I'm gonna enjoy\n
myself.
Ugh...
As Kuon and I reach the main plaza, we find it\n
already packed with people...
Looks like everyone is clamoring to get a\n
glimpse of the imperial princess.
I should've expected this place would be\n
packed...
Hey, Kuon.
What is it?
Can I go home? All these people around me are\n
making me claustrophobic...
At this rate, we're not even gonna get a look\n
at the shrine, let alone the princess.
I think... I think I may have misjudged the\n
situation.
A particularly loud cheer in the distance\n
interrupts us.
It seems as though the princess's palanquin is\n
fast approaching...
So, what do we do? If we stay here, the only\n
view we're getting is the back of this guy's\n
head.
And then an idea occurs to me.
Hey, hang on. The parade goes all the way around\n
the capital, right? Does it go to the Mausoleum?
I would imagine so...
That's where she's supposed to step off the\n
shrine to greet the cityfolk.
Wouldn't it be better for us to wait over\n
there, then?
Maybe, but the parade travels slowly. It'll\n
take a while for it to get over there.
Even better. It's a pretty big place, and if\n
it'll be a while yet, it shouldn't be too\n
crowded.
We came all the way here for this, and I'm gonna\n
be disappointed if we don't actually SEE\n
anything.
I won't be satisfied until I can marvel at this\n
grand "imperial flower" you keep talking up.
Heh, I suppose you're right. We did come all\n
this way.
Oh, but first...
If it's going to take a while, let's get some\n
food first.
*Sigh*...
Ultimately, we make our way out of the crowded\n
plaza and on to the Mausoleum.
It's a much wider area than the plaza, and not\n
nearly as mobbed--at least not yet.
This ought to be a good spot.
Agreed.
We manage to find a spot near the front, then\n
settle in to wait for the parade with our food.
As I glance over to where the princess is\n
supposed to arrive in front of the Mausoleum...
I spy a row of intimidating figures assembled\n
there, as though waiting for the palanquin.
They have to be mononofu of some kind--\n
all of them are carrying weapons.
Who're those guys over there?
Kuon follows my gaze to the figures, then\n
nods knowingly.
The Eight Pillar Generals.
I suppose all eight of them have assembled to\n
meet the princess.
So that's them...
Mhm. Just as their name implies, they're the\n
pillars that uphold the empire's military\n
might...
Vurai the Vanguard...
He's extremely devoted to the Mikado, even for\n
a general, and is renowned for his raw strength.
Rumor is that his Akuruka was granted to him\n
because he showed astounding battlefield\n
prowess.
An Akuruka...? You mean like Oshtor's mask?
Yep. They're something of a symbol of power in\n
Yamato.
Not even all of the Pillars have them.\n
The Mikado has to recognize you as a peerless\n
warrior, first.
I see...
Raiko the Sage...
Word is he's not very skilled with weapons,\n
but he's an unmatched master of tactics and\n
strategy.
Supposedly, he's taken entire strongholds\n
without a fight. Not a single drop of blood\n
spilled, I think.
Capturing a fortress without fighting an actual\n
battle? Is he some kind of wizard?
He's also the elder brother of the Imperial\n
Guard of the Left, Lord Mikazuchi.
A family of bigwigs, eh...
Soyankekur the Mariner...
He's Shyahoro's ruling owlo, but also commands\n
Yamato's seas. All maritime trade goes through\n
him.
Not to mention he's a naval combat expert.\n
There's a reason pirates don't operate in\n
Shyahoro waters.
So that's Atuy's father... She really is a\n
bona fide princess, then.
...I can see the resemblance, I guess.
Ozen the Harvester...
He fights well, but more important are the\n
agricultural miracles he works as owlo of the\n
wastelands.
And he's also Rulutieh's father.
Who would've thought that a dainty little thing\n
like Rulutieh is this guy's daughter? He's\n
terrifying...
Tokifusa the Attuner...
He's not... really renowned for anything\n
specific, but he adapts easily to all sorts\n
of tactics.
He's usually sent to put down rebellions, and\n
holds the line for the more specialized Pillars.
He must be skilled, to adapt to any role like\n
that--but next to the others, he doesn't stand\n
out.
He wields a bow that's longer than his own\n
height. Maybe Oshtor could set up a sparring\n
match for you?
Yeah, no thanks.
Woshis the Shadowlight...
He acts as a mediator for the other generals,\n
mostly. His serene personality is perfect for\n
it.
Huh... Could be my imagination, but have I seen\n
that guy before?
I don't know much about his past, but if he's\n
a Pillar, he must have the Mikado's utmost\n
trust.
...Dekopompo.
Urgh, right. He's one of the Eight Pillars too,\n
isn't he?
Pretty sure you already know all you NEED to\n
know about him.
Welp. You don't have any other info on the\n
guy?
Not a lot. There's not much out there,\n
honestly--I don't know why he's a Pillar to\n
begin with.
I totally forgot that windbag is one of the\n
Eight Pillar Generals. Must be a pillar full\n
of bugs and rot...
And that leaves... oh, doesn't look like she's\n
up there, but her name is Munechika the\n
Guardian.
Her specialty is defensive strategy, and she's\n
defended entire nations from barbarians.
The Mikado bestowed an Akuruka upon her when\n
her extraordinary abilities became apparent.
Munechika must be accompanying the princess,\n
since she bears the duty of acting as her\n
guardian.
Makes sense...
I'm surprised you know all this.
You know their names, matched their faces--\n
you even knew about Raiko and Mikazuchi being\n
related.
Have you been gathering info on them this\n
whole time?
I-I just... enjoy learning, I suppose?
That so?
Y-Yes! It pays to know things. You need to\n
stop being so ignorant of all this, Haku.
The Eight Pillar Generals are all household\n
names! Even children know them by heart.
Yes!
...Oh! Look, the parade is coming!
Oh...
A loud cheer goes up around us as I catch a\n
glimpse of the mikoshi--the mobile shrine--\n
not far off.
It's hard to make out her face, but that\n
general walking out in front must be\n
Munechika...
And Oshtor and Mikazuchi have taken up their\n
positions on either side as the Twin Shields.
It's easy to forget Oshtor is a proper noble\n
sometimes. But when I see him like this...
Yeah.
It makes me think the Oshtor we actually know\n
is just a dream.
The portable shrine comes to a stop, and the\n
princess descends to meet the Eight Pillar\n
Generals.
Here she comes...
Now then, let's see what the beloved princess\n
actually looks like.
I lean forward toward the shrine to get a\n
better look...
Whoa...
I can't help but exclaim as I lay eyes on the\n
elegant, glittering clothing she's wearing...
...but I can't quite see her face.
A decorative cloth covers up most of her face--\n
I can't see a thing, no features, no\n
expression...
Her frame is very small, though. She must still\n
be a child.
The regal princess stops before the crowd to\n
raise her hand, and the people around me go\n
wild.
All eyes are on the princess--not just the\n
crowd's, but the Pillars' and the other\n
authorities', too.
Seems she isn't fazed a bit by all the crowds.\n
I guess I should expect as much from an\n
imperial princess...
As I jostle to try and get a look at her face,\n
she disappears into the Mausoleum with her\n
retainers.
Damn. I was able to see her wave, at least,\n
if not her face... I guess we should head back\n
now.
Kuon?
You see something you like?
No, it's nothing. Come on, let's get going.
It was hard to tell with the princess, but the\n
presence those Eight Pillar Generals had...
It terrified me, in a way. Makes me glad I'm\n
just a nobody.
Hope I never get on their bad side.
Haku?
Sorry, yeah, let's head back. Want to hit those\n
food stalls again on the way?
That sounds like a great idea, I think.
Whew... Man, I'm full.
It's getting on toward late afternoon...
We all relished Rulutieh's cooking for lunch,\n
and now, we're slowly making our way back home.
Now all I have to do is contrive a way to sneak\n
in a power nap where Kuon and Nekone can't find\n
me...
They're catching onto my secret technique of\n
sleeping with my eyes open. Time for a new\n
tactic.
Hm?
I open the door to our base of operations to\n
find a girl I've never seen before...
She's lazily draped over the couch and reading\n
some kind of book.
And she has snacks and a drink just within\n
arm's reach. She certainly looks relaxed...
Because of her position, her skirt is splayed\n
wide, baring everything underneath.
She hums innocently, flipping the page of her\n
book, and reaches for another bite of her\n
snacks...
She's just... lying there. As I watch, she takes\n
a swig of her drink, indulging herself.
...and now she's scratching her butt.
Truly, I behold a paragon of lazing around.
...My bad.
Yikes. Almost made a fool of myself, going into\n
the wrong... Wait.
I look around to make sure. This definitely is\n
our room.
I don't THINK I'm wrong, at least?
What's the matter?
You're not gonna go inside?
There's, uh, a girl I don't know in there.\n
Butt and all.
Sir Haku...?
What are you even talking about?
Hold on, hold on. Rulutieh, would you stop\n
looking at me like I'm crazy?
Rulutieh's gaze in particular digs into me.
...Maybe I just imagined it.
There's no way there'd just be a random\n
uninvited girl in our r--
Who the hell?
Ah, you've returned!
I've waited upon your arrival for quite some\n
time, now. You must be the Haku I've heard\n
so much about.
The stranger tosses aside her book and sits up\n
to greet us.
W-Wait, that book...
I can hear the tremble in Rulutieh's voice\n
behind me.
Hm? Something about this book bothers you?\n
I chanced upon it, I admit, hidden in the\n
lining of--
A-Ah!
In a flash, Rulutieh darts into the room and\n
snatches the book, holding it to her chest\n
tightly.
What... the...?
I gaze in amazement. This doesn't seem like the\n
Rulutieh I know...
I must say, that volume's contents are\n
fascinating indeed. I'd never heard of such\n
tales.
The depths of friendship shared by two men...\n
they are touching stories indeed.
Tell me, have you the sequel it promises?\n
I would have you bring it, that I may read more.
Ah... u-uhm... ah...
Could you perhaps explain this particular\n
passage to me? The one about "his excited m-"
Aaa! AAAA! AAAAAAA!!
Wh--
Why is she yelling to drown the kid out?\n
I've never seen Rulutieh like this before...
What exactly has gotten into her?
Rulutieh?
Eep!
Rulutieh jumps at the sound of my voice.
*Krrrk*...
She slowly turns her head like a rusty screw,\n
her cheeks red and her eyes full of tears.
She's holding that book to her chest like she's\n
hiding it, but what could possible be so emb--
Oh.\n
I think I get it now.
Hey, Rulutieh, it's nothing to be embarrassed by.\n
It's only natural to be interested in that stuff.
Actually, I've never read the ones meant for\n
women. I'm sort of interested in what they're\n
like.
Huh? Now she's getting even redder than\n
before...
...S-Sir Haku is... i-interested in...???\n
But that's--
Rulutieh covers her face and barrels out of the\n
room as quickly as she can.
...What?
Wow, Haku. You must be quite the sadist, to\n
take her apart in so few words.
...I don't get it.
What are you all mumbling about?\n
Can you not see I've RUN OUT of snacks?\n
I order you to bring more.
More? What!?
Those were supposed to be for EVERYONE!\n
A-And is that the mead I was saving!?
Urp. Yes, it was quite palatable.
Urgh, this girl reeks...
It's really hard to come by this st--a-and\n
you're supposed to water it down, and--I was\n
looking forwar--
*Thump*...
Kuon sinks to the ground, in utter shock.
D-Dear sister?
Kuon...
Oh, dear... she's rather torn up, isn't she?
You lot are quite... what's the word?
Insolent, yes. How dare you enjoy such\n
delicacies, when I've never tasted their ilk?\n
Preposterous.
I DEMAND seconds. Hurry along, now. I command it.
Th-This girl--No, it can't be...\n
Can it?
Kiwru, who'd been watching the whole fiasco\n
unfold in silence, begins muttering in\n
disbelief.
Friend of yours, Kiwru?
W-Well... no, it's not that, just...
Enough tarrying! I ordered you to be about it\n
QUICKLY, did I not?
Silently, Nekone pushes the gibbering Kiwru\n
aside and steps forward.
Please, Your Highness. I humbly request that you\n
mind yourself amongst those of lower station.
What?
Your Highness...?
Oh, no. Oh, no, no, it really IS her!
Kiwru suddenly flings himself to the floor,\n
bowed so low his head nearly touches the\n
floorboards.
Ah!
Gah!? Kiwru, what the hell are you bowing for\n
all of a sudden--
What are YOU doing? You're in the presence of\n
the princess, Haku! Bow down!
A princess?
But... you're a prince, too. So is she\n
another princess like Rulutieh, or...?
Please, d-don't draw such base comparisons!
This is the exalted Mikado's own blood--\n
his only daughter, the Imperial Princess\n
and Divine Scion Anju.
The... imperial princess...
It can't be...
Oh, wow, I didn't recognize her. I KNEW I'd met\n
her before! That's definitely the princess,\n
love.
She spends so much time all cooped up, I'd\n
forgotten what her face looks like!
How could you even BEGIN to forget something\n
that important...?
Why are you all just idly ch-chatting!? We are\n
in the PRESENCE of HER IMPERIAL HIGHNESS.
Please, be at ease. This day, I wear the guise\n
of a mere city girl, not a princess.\n
Understood?
Dispense with such overblown formalities,\n
I implore you.
I don't imagine you came here on your own...
Of course I came alone! A lowly commoner girl\n
hardly struts about flanked by guards.
Alone...
Alas, you needn't worry. I did leave a note\n
before I made my timely escape and snuck out.
Snuck... out...
Kiwru collapses into the fetal position,\n
clutching his stomach. Is he still hungry\n
after lunch..?
Do you care to explain why you came here,\n
Highness? I can't begin to fathom why...
Ah, yes, yes. I'd nearly forgotten in the\n
excitement.
There is but one reason I descend upon your inn\n
this eve: Oshtor!
Oshtor?
Why's she talking about Oshtor?\n
Did he do something?
Indeed, the KNAVE Oshtor claims he has no time\n
to speak with me in the palace when I entreat\n
him.
So, I have come to seek him out on my own terms.
Truly, I do SO much to express myself to him,\n
and he seems to take no notice...
So Oshtor is the princess's favorite or\n
something? Unless it's more like...
Nekone is slowly losing all emotion in her\n
face...
It's actually scaring me a little.\n
I wish she'd stop.
So? Speak words to me. Where is Oshtor hiding?\n
I demand to know.
There's hardly any reason for my dear--for\n
Oshtor to be here. Now, if you'd be so kind,\n
Highness--scram.
Wh--Nekone!?
You cannot fool me so easily. I've already\n
surmised that YOU'RE somehow connected with\n
Oshtor.
Now bring him to me, posthaste.\n
I command it.
Hold on! Hold on one second. You keep asking us\n
to bring him here, but that's impossible.
First of all, Oshtor's not even here. We can\n
hardly bring you someone who isn't in the\n
building.
Second, it's true we're doing work for him, but\n
that's supposed to be classified.
Him coming here would be a big red flag.\n
We can't let people know he's connected to us.
Typically, we go to him, whenever he has a new\n
job for us.
You--
Of course, he does put in appearances here as\n
Ukon, but I'm not about to tell her that.
So, no matter how much you may want to see him,\n
you're not going to find him here.
*Grrr...*
Haku! HAKU!
Yeah?
Her Highness may have granted us permission,\n
but still, to speak to her in such a way...
...I guess I see your point, but eh. Whatever.
H-Haku!?
Now that she knows she won't find Oshtor here,\n
I doubt we'll see her again anyway...
Are you done talking, then?
Urk...
Kuon's voice is no different from its usual,\n
kind tones, but why did I just feel a chill\n
down my spine?
Kuon slowly rises behind Anju...
*Sshf*...
Eh? What is the meaning of this?
And an all-too-familiar, rope-like length wraps\n
around Anju's head.
*Krkk*...
Kuon's tail starts to tighten around Anju's\n
forehead...
I don't know quite how tight she can make it,\n
but I'm definitely starting to hear weird\n
sounds.
A-Aagh! AAAAA!!
Rulutieh poured her heart and soul into\n
preparing those snacks you devoured, you know.
And you washed it all down with the mead I was\n
specially saving...
You even drank it straight. Such a waste...
*Krkk*... *krrrkkk*...
A-Ah--Wh-Who do you think I am, knave!?\n
Unhand me at once!
Just a random city girl, aren't you?
Surely the honorable princess of Yamato wouldn't\n
go back on her word just because she's in a\n
bind.
Urgh...
And I would certainly think she wouldn't be so\n
petty as to use her rank for leverage over\n
others...
*Krrkk*... *KRRRKKKK*...
Nnngh...
And it's most CERTAINLY my duty as this little\n
city girl's elder to scold her when she's out\n
of line.
What do we say when we've done something\n
wrong?
*Krrkk*...
I... I have... done noth--!!
*Krrrrkkkk*....
Ow, ow, ow--I-I am--the scion of--the heavens!\n
W-Were I to lie, the very HONOR of Yamato--
*Krrkkk krkk krk*--
What's the magic word?
F-for me, there is no retreat, no begging,\n
no remorse!
Ahahaa...
Kuon smiles widely.
*Krrkk*... *KRRKKK*--
She makes fists with both her hands, then\n
places them on either side of Anju's head.
*Grind, grind--*
Gaaahh!!
Whaaat's the magic wooord?
*Grind, grind, grind*...
Hhhhgh--
Stop, stop, stop! That's enough, Kuon.
I quickly step in to stop Kuon before she can\n
squeeze any harder.
I'm pretty sure doing any more could be really\n
bad.
The fact that Anju's head is starting to look\n
like an hourglass is probably a bad sign...
Haku...
Yeah, I think she's learned her lesson.
Atuy?
Really, Kuon, it isn't like you to get so\n
worked up over something so trivial.
Of course she has not learned her lesson.\n
It is for her own good. Let us see how long\n
she lasts...
I think there's someone whispering behind me,\n
but it's probably just my imagination.
I do understand how you feel, Kuon...
Atuy smiles meaningfully.
Hee. I guess I'll take one for the team, then.
...What do you mean by that?
Weeeell, love. It just so happens I'm prepared\n
for this sort of development!
She moves toward a shelf and puts her hand\n
against it, beginning to push...
*Grrrnnn...*
The entire shelf slides over to one side, and\n
behind it--a small hidden compartment!
Wow. I never knew there was a mechanism like\n
that, hiding in such plain sight...
Kuon finally unwraps her tail from Anju's head,\n
impressed by the shelf.
To tell the truth, I found it completely by\n
accident, but I've been using it for my secret\n
stash.
Just as she reaches inside the compartment,\n
however, she stops.
Eh?
What's going on here? Where's my special stash\n
of drinks?
Special?
That's so strange. I had some expensive stuff\n
in here, too...
Where is it? Where did it go!?
Haku? You look a little pale. Is something\n
I-It's, uh. It's nothing.
It's not nothing.
A while back, the other guys and I got together\n
for drinks at home base, but we ended up\n
running out.
We were looking around, trying to find at least\n
one spare bottle, when I tripped and moved the\n
shelf...
Gah!? What's this?
Oh ho! Mine eyes doth behold a trove of finest\n
libations--and how their siren song tempteth...
Well, would you look at that. We could start\n
all over and still have drinks left.
I don't know. Who does this all belong to,\n
anyway...?
Don't be such a stiff, Kiwru. It's in our base,\n
so it's ours to drink, isn't it?
Bwahaha! I like the way you think, kid.
Gentle lords, I believe we have arrived at a\n
concord most splendid. Let us unto our revels!
And so we ended up drinking all of it. We never\n
replaced the bottles we took, either...
The only other one in the room who knows the\n
truth is Kiwru. I look over to him quickly--
He looks back to me, trying to mouth something.\n
His hands are on his stomach like he's in pain.
Yeah. The best plan would be to pretend we\n
don't know anything.
It looks like Kiwru is desperately trying to\n
communicate something to me, but I pretend not\n
to notice.
We have to stay quiet, Kiwru.
Atuy and Kuon, meanwhile--having no idea what\n
we did--turn to the most likely culprit.
...N-Now, hold! I'm innocent of whatever crime\n

*Krrrkkk*...
And Kuon's tail, of course, finds its way\n
around Anju's head once again.
Hurgh--
A proper ruler must learn not to take things\n
that aren't hers, I think, hmm?
*Krrkk*, *krrkkkk*...
Owowow--th-this is all a great misunderstanding,\n
please! I had no idea of--
*Krrrkkkk*...
This is starting to eat at my conscience.\n
At this rate, things could quickly get out of\n
hand...
Uh, Kuon? I don't think she meant\n
any harm. She's just a kid...
*Krrrkkkk... krkk*, *krrkkk*...
She's not listening to a word I say.\n
Great.
Now is the time to dethrone this foul tyrant!
Suddenly, Atuy produces her spear and puts the\n
blade against Anju's ear!
H-Hold it! Just hold it, would you? This is\n
spiraling out of control--
Evil shall pay!
Before I can stop her, she drags her nails down\n

screech.
EEEEEEEEEE--
GYAAAH!!
--EEEEEEEEE--
My ears! My ears!!
The hellish punishment only continues, until...
Anju is left limp on the ground, her will to\n

I hope you've learned your lesson.
Kuon slowly begins to loosen her tail's grasp...
Y-Yes, yes. I'll apologize. I'll a-apologize,\n
so please, release me!
I can let you go then, I suppose.
Kuon finally relents, letting go of Anju's head.
Ungh...
Anju crumbles, holding her aching head, tears\n
brewing in her eyes...
Then, in a flash, she bolts for the door.
As if I would ever apologize to the likes of\n
you, cretin! Drunkard! I bet you'll never find\n
a husband!
*NYOOM*
Ah, well. There she goes. I'll just have to\n
dole out more punishment if she turns up\n

Wh-What have we done... to Her H-Highness...?
Kiwru mutters to himself with lifeless eyes\n

I-If word of this ever gets out...
No need to worry. She's just a plain city girl,\n

I made EXTRA sure she'll remember that.
Kuon smiles smugly.
So that's why she said it like that...
Impressive as always, dear sister.
Nekone seems much more upbeat than usual.
Hee hee. Well, if she's just a commoner,\n
there's no harm in teaching her a lesson,\n
I guess.
If I recall correctly, I'm fairly sure Atuy\n
was trying to prevent all this...
Urgh... My stomach...
...I'm keeping this secret to my grave.
Uuaahh... so sleepy...
Kuon found me napping when I was supposed to be\n
doing accounting work, so she sent me on\n
errands.
Rulutieh is sewing and the others are all busy,\n
so I'm the only one available to go out...
I was under the impression Kiwru is supposed\n
to do these random errands. Why is it always\n
me?
And Atuy tottered off on her own, claiming to\n
be patrolling the city, of course.
Urf. I'm pretty sure that's everything taken\n

As I walk, I double-check the list of tasks\n

Yeah, looks good.
This took a lot longer than I thought.\n
Better head back.
Don't want Kuon to think I'm slacking off, or\n
else she'll cut my allowance again...
I can't really talk back when I owe her so\n
much, but man, who does she think she is,\n
my mother?
Hm?
Halfway back to the Hakurokaku Inn, I stop as\n

This fragrant smell of sauce...\n
It must be from a kokoromo skewer.
Kokoromo dishes use the meat of a packbeast\n
that's usually slaughtered for fur and bone.
It has a pungent scent and tends to be sinewy,\n
but when prepared well, it's a flavorful meal.
*Grrmbl*...
The smell the cooking skewers give off is\n
almost criminally tempting.
I take out my wallet and check it carefully...
I should have enough for a skewer or two.\n
It'll be a while until dinner, so a snack is\n
in order...
Man's voice
Look, missy, that ain't gonna fly with me.
As I follow my nose to the stall, an agitated,\n
confused voice fades into my hearing.
What, pray tell, will not fly?
Eh?
Give it a rest, already! I'm a busy man,\n
y'know!
Ah, in that case, pay me no mind. You may\n
continue your work uninterrupted.
It is, after all, my duty as a ruler to mind\n
the will and comfort of my people.
Look, missy, will you quit spouting nonsense\n

It is you who spouts nonsense, sir. What is\n
this "pay up" you speak of?
You ate my skewers, yeah? A whole damn bunch\n
of 'em?
Indeed. I must say, your flavoring techniques\n
are crude, but it was a satisfying meal.\n
My compliments.
So now comes the part where you pay for what\n
you ate.
...Pay?
Aaaargh! Would somebody please do something\n
about this?
The deeper voice must be the meat vendor.\n
And I have a sinking feeling I know the other\n
one...
I really want to know what the hell she's\n
doing here. I've got a bad feeling about this...
...I'm gonna pretend I didn't hear anything.
I better get away from here before she ropes\n
me into--
Hm? Ah, you there.
Urgh...
Crap, she noticed...
Oshtor's servant boy, aren't you?\n
Well met.
What exactly are you doing here?\n
And hey, wh-who are you calling "servant boy!?"
Your name was... Saku? Or was it Deku?
It's Haku.
Ah, yes. Yaku.
I said Haku!
Man
Hey, you. You know this girl?
Huh? I-I--in the technical sense, I guess, sure--
Then knock some sense into her!
This girl's eaten a whole mess of skewers and\n
she's tryin' to get outta paying for 'em.
I'm runnin' a business, here! I can't go\n
giving out free meals. I gotta eat too,\n
you know.
I look at Anju's feet to find an astronomical\n
number of skewers scattered on the cobbles.
Just how much DID she eat...?
Hey.
What is it?
Those skewers on the ground. Are those all from\n
what you ate?
Indeed. I was finally able to escape my\n
chambers again, so I decided to wander the\n
capital.
And as I walked past this place, I found myself\n
enchanted by these skewers' arresting\n
fragrance.
I could hardly help myself when this kind vendor\n
presented me with what he was cooking, you see.
It would have been rude to reject such an offer,\n
and now he refuses to cease talking about\n
"paying."
Is this true?
Well, she kept lookin' over, yeah?\n
So I asked her if she'd like one.
She ate the thing in an instant and demanded\n
more.
I asked if she could pay, and she said not to\n
worry...
And then the moment she's done, she says she's\n
got no idea what I'm talkin' about. You get me?
All right, I think I understand what's going\n
on here.
Hey, Anju. Do you know what money is?
No, what is that?
Ever hear the words, uh.\n
"Accounting?" Or "payment?"
Your speech is foreign to me. For your sake,\n
I hope you aren't trying to make a fool of me...
Looks like this princess is so secluded and of\n
such high station, nobody's told her how money\n
works.
Well, you see...
With that in mind, I begin to explain to her\n

I see. So this man is requesting a reward for\n

That's not quite--you know what?\n
Yes. Yes, that's exactly what he's asking for.
Hm. I suppose I understand, but ordinarily I\n
only reward those of the highest distinction...
Ah, no matter. It is quite true that you've\n
entertained me, sir. I'll consider this an\n

Anju reaches into her pockets to withdraw\n

Whoa...
She holds up a glittering gold necklace,\n

Hm, this should do.
Why are you just standing there? I cannot\n

Wh--I-I can't accept something like that!
Yeah, thought so.
Even at a glance, I can tell it's artisanal\n
work. It'd fetch an extremely high price.
No matter how many skewers you scarf down,\n
it'd never match the amount of money this is\n
worth.
And to accept it as payment for such a\n

Anyone would be looking for the catch.
It's not difficult to imagine heads literally\n
rolling over this necklace, if it's the real\n
deal.
Only someone with a lot of guts or a complete\n

He seems to realize as much, sizing up the\n

He leans toward me and whispers quietly.
Hey, buddy. You said you know this girl?\n
Who exactly is she?
No idea. Probably some rich family's kid.
Whatever. How much you got on you?
Huh? I don't have nearly enough to pay for what\n

I pull out my wallet to check, and the vendor\n

One, two, three... eh, not exactly enough,\n

He shoves my wallet back into my hands, empty.
Hold on just a minute--
Come on over, folks! Step right up for cheap,\n

The vendor pretends not to hear me, going back\n

Damn it all! My allowance... How did it come\n

Well, where are we going next?
WE'RE not going anywhere. I'M going home--\n
and why are you still following me!?
I have arrived at the conclusion that your\n

Now that you've mentioned it, visiting your\n

Seems you've already forgotten what happened\n

Ah, but now I'm reminded. Those delicacies\n
I sampled on my previous visit, what are they\n
called?
I had never eaten, nor even laid eyes upon\n

I attempted to describe them to my cook, but\n
she assured me no such things exist!\n
Preposterous.
Oh, that stuff was a recreation of something I...\n
sort of remember?
I'm pretty sure it can't be found in this\n
country, anyway. I wouldn't blame your cook.
Ah, is that so? I'm relieved to hear it.
Relieved?
The imperial cook is considered the greatest\n

That there might have been a dish she knew\n

Apparently she felt so ashamed, she offered to\n

I managed to convince her otherwise, but it\n

Wh--Yikes. Holy shit.
In any event, I have a wonderful proposal for\n

What? No. I'm going home, and so are you,\n

I go through all the trouble of escaping the\n

Look, consider your own position. Do you have\n

I admit you have a point, but I remind you:\n
I am the heir to the Mikado, and will one day\n

If I am to rule with any kind of wisdom, I must\n

That is why I venture out into the city.\n
To witness for myself the people I must\n

I see. So you're actually thinking of Yamato's\n

Of course. What kind of fool do you take me\n

You expect me to buy that story? It's obvious\n

You DARE level such false accusations at me!?
Yeah, I figured.
Y-You have no proof of such wild--
Why don't you try looking at me? That's a habit\n

lie.
I AM looking at you. Wh-What habit is this you\n

She's still playing dumb after all this?
M-More importantly, you will obey at once!\n
Guide me around the capital. The princess\n

Hold on a minu--bwuh!
How is she this strong? I can't stop her--
*Drag, drag*...
L-Let go of me! I said let go--
Tell me, what is that there? What is its\n

Why does it always end up like this...
That? Oh, that's a show-tent.
A show-tent?
You've never heard of them? They're public\n

I see. Similar to the Imperial Theater.
They do occasionally put on plays, yes, but...\n

Oh? My interest is piqued. Let us enter.
Not happening.
Why is that!?
My wallet's completely empty. I can't pay for\n

Tch. Would this not suffice?
Anju produces the necklace again.
No, not that, please.
Ah, what a lively place THIS is!\n
So many different kinds of fruits, sweets...
This is the place that keeps the capital's\n

I would expect no less from Yamato. This is\n

Anju makes the proclamation proudly and\n

She sniffs the air as though picking out a\n

*Sizzle, sizzle*...
You've got to be kidding. She just ate a ton.
Ah, it seems they rotate the meat as they roast\n

Yeah, seems like it.
Damn, but that smells good. Now I'M starting\n

Its scent differs from the skewers I sampled\n

Seems like it.
So it is different from the dish called\n

With that, you shave off the meat that's cooked\n

What are you talking about? I've never seen\n
such a dish. It's eaten directly from one's\n

She's never seen it before? Are the meals\n

And what of that one? The one adjacent to it?
That's cooked karakatsua. It's cooked whole.\n
I doubt it's really up to your refined tastes...
*Grumble, grumble*...
Would you--
No money, remember? I'm completely broke.
Would you quit flashing that thing?
And so, Anju drags me all around the capital...
At long last, we reach the edge of the capital--\n
the city gates. The sunset has turned the sky\n

It's this late already? We must've spent a wh--\n
aw, crap. I forgot I'm supposed to be running\n
I'm getting a headache just thinking about the\n
telling-off Kuon is going to give me...
...but I guess my first problem is what to do\n

I'm sure she's already caused some alarm in the\n
court, and the last thing I want to do is\n

I'm the one being dragged around, but that's\n
not what it's going to look like if I'm\n

Over there...
What lies beyond those gates?
Beyond the...? A lot of things, I guess.\n
Mountains, rivers, plains, other countries...\n
the ocean.
The ocean...
I'm told the water stretches as far as the eye\n

You've never seen it?
I have... never been beyond the bounds of the\n
imperial capital.
I've not the slightest idea of what other\n

...I see.
I knew she was brought up sheltered, but I had\n

No wonder she wants to leave her cage.\n
I guess her line about the people of the city\n

'Course, it still sucks that I got roped into\n

My knowledge of the outside world consists\n

Oshtor?
Her voice, full of loneliness, brightens a bit\n

Indeed. Oshtor has shared with me a great many\n

How beautiful the views outside are...
How the people of this country live...
The strange and varied creatures of the wilds...
Anju's eyes begin to fill with tears as she\n

...He used to tell me... so much...
Damn it, Oshtor, look what you've done.\n
You make her sad by leaving her alone, and now\n

You there, young man. How dare you make such\n

Hey, hold up. I'm not the one who made her cr--\n
Huh?
I glance behind me to find that old candy vendor\n

The candy vendor...
Old man
Look who it is. Not only were you dragging those\n

I told you, I'm not the one who made her cry.
Bah. That she's saddened in your company makes\n

Hey, that's not fair.

A sweet ought to help cheer her up.
Sorry, but I actually don't have any money\n

Not only do you make a girl cry, but you're\n

Nngh...
I can't really argue with him, but what did\n

Here, girl. Take this. I don't know what's made\n

Candy!
Anju's face immediately brightens as she sees\n

But this is so beautiful! I've never seen such\n

Hohoho, you flatter me! And every word is true,\n

Gh... I can't put my finger on why, but he's\n

Hm? You want one too, boy? Well, since you\n

The old man tosses something my way,\n

This gigiri thing again.
It's starting to get darker, so I decide to\n

Hey, about what you were saying earlier.
You mean...
About Oshtor.
Oh.

N-Nah, I don't really have much of a sweet\n

Urgh... I'm getting chills down my spine...
Mm! This one is quite delicious as well.
G-Glad to hear it...
This was... quite delightful.
Tell me, have you any aspirations of serving me\n

I find myself taken with your brazenness.\n

O-Oh. Right. I completely forgot she's the\n

So? What say you?
U-Uhm, I don't know. This is... pretty sudden.

As we talk, we find ourselves at our\n

Truly, it is so.

We will meet again.
Sure...
She's planning on escaping again, isn't she...
It's extremely late by the time I make it back\n

Hey, I'm back.
And where have you been out to so late,\n

Kuon smiles unsettlingly as she "welcomes" me\n

Nngh. THAT princess again, huh?

Uh.

Haku...
Look, please, this whole day has been out of\n

Take a seat.
Just let me explain!
I said take a seat.
...Yes, ma'am.
Whew, glad that's over with.

I mean, yeah, that's LITERALLY a dirty job,\n

Would you please stop complaining?\n

You fail to recall that WE were working as\n

True, but think about it. Who'd you prefer to\n

What's it gonna be?
That's...
If you don't want to, I can get it myself.\n
I'll make enough for everyone, but no\n

My tea-brewing skills are mediocre at best,\n

I... can just get my own tea.
That so? Then pour me a cup too while you're\n

Wha--Why does it come back to that!?\n

It can't be... Is the great scholar Nekone\n

Rrrgh...
Haku, I know it's fun to tease Nekone because\n

Aheh heh....
Dear sister...
Perfect timing, Rulutieh. Could you brew us\n

Haku, need I remind you, LADY Rulutieh is the\n

Um... I don't mind...

Um... I-I'm sorry I couldn't cook them myself\n

Hm? Wait, these are--?

Hee... yes, let's.

I-I'll assist you, then. It would be improper\n

Kuon's tail sways happily as she speaks.\n

Hee hee, snack time, is it?

I guess that's not totally out-of-character\n

Ah, you've returned. Forgive my intrusion,\n

Huh?
To my disbelief, I return to find a person who\n

She shouldn't be here, yet there she sits,\n
