What's so bad about that? You've been doing well\n
so far. Don't sweat the small stuff like this.
...Seems like every time we talk, I can't help\n
but think about how Ukon is so different from\n
Oshtor.
Oh, and make sure Nosuri doesn't hear a word\n
of this, of course.
What a goddamn farce...
To be honest, this job IS as much of a pain\n
in the ass as I thought, but I can't go back\n
on my word now.
Forget waiting around. Let's just go find her\n
and "accidentally" drive her off so we can be\n
done.
She wouldn't make a huge fuss of it, I bet.\n
In fact, she'd likely run just as soon as she's\n
spotted.
That's easier said than done.
Yeah, I know, I know...
According to Ukon's info, she plans on using the\n
sewers, but if she goes a different route...
Are you sure we shouldn't split up? Cast a\n
wider net?
It'll be difficult to respond to any changes in\n
the situation if we spread ourselves out too\n
thin.
I'm not trying to just keep you all around just\n
so I can take it easy. Definitely not.
...I should have figured.
As we continue whispering, milling aimlessly\n
about the back alley... IT happens.
*FWUMPH*
AHH!?
The ground under me gives way, and for a single\n
terrifying moment, I stand on nothing over an\n
abyss.
Haku!?
Before I can take Kuon's outstretched hand and\n
pull myself to safety, I fall into the sewer.
AHHHHHH!!
Flailing blindly as I plummet, I reach out and\n
desperately grab hold of the first thing I can--
*THUMP--*
What the--!?
G-Gah--falling--!!
I don't know what I grabbed onto, but it halts\n
my fall. Even so, I start slipping, unsteady at\n
best.
Beneath me gapes an enormous hole into the\n
sewers. It's a LONG fall. Panicking, I hold on\n
for dear life.
Urgh--what are y--Unhand me!!
*FWUMP {W35}WHOOSH {W35}FWOOSH {W35}WH--*
Wh--!? Quit flailing, or I'm gonna fall!
I tighten my grasp on whatever it is that had\n
broken my fall, holding on with both hands...
Something soft brushes past my hand, and with\n
a ripping sound, I begin to fall again.
BWAH!?
Wh-Wh-Where do you think you're grabbing!?\n
LET GO!
You've gotta be joking! If I let go, I fall to\n
my death!
As much as I cling to safety, the flailing keeps\n
me from climbing any higher to get a secure\n
grip.
I said let go!
*FWUMP {W35}FWUMP {W35}FWUMP {W35}FWOOSH--*
Stop! STOP!! This is no laughing matter, OK?\n
I'm SERIOUSLY gonna--
*Slip*
My right hand slips, and suddenly I drop another\n
foot, hanging by a strip of cloth.
Even that begins to rip with my entire weight\n
pulling on it... Gotta grab onto something\n
else!
*Rustle, rustle--{W120} slip*
I quickly try to seize what looks like someone's\n
waist, but grab only cloth, dropping another\n
foot.
YAHHH!?
And then I'm immediately up again, faster than\n
I can blink, the cloth going taut.
For a moment, I... probably saw something I\n
shouldn't have, but I can't tell because of the\n
dark.
Must be my eyes playing tricks.
Y-Y-You SCOUNDREL! Unhand me NOW!!\n
Let go! Let GO, LET--!!
Though my "rescuer" holds the bunch of cloth\n
up with all her might, my weight drags on it...
Guh... urgh... HhhhaaAAAHH!!
She pulls upward with a surge of strength!
Gah... Urk... C-Can't... get... a good... grip...!!
Distracted as she is with having to keep\n
BOTH of us on the ladder with one hand, her\n
grip falters.
As we continue struggling, it dawns on me\n
exactly what just happened:
The person we've been waiting to emerge from\n
the sewers opened the manhole in that back\n
alley.
I happened to be walking right over it at the\n
time, and fell straight in, grabbing onto her.
...or onto her clothes, more accurately.\n
Regardless, I feel kinda bad for what I've done.
Whatever the circumstances, I have zero intention\n
of letting go. If I let go, I fall into the\n
death pit.
Ack.
An ominous sound fills my ears.
The cloth covering my savior begins tearing in\n
earnest, and I see things I probably shouldn't.
*THWACK!*
Hnngah!?
A foot connects with my head as she tries to\n
kick me away, face blazing red.
GET. {W50}OFF. {W40}LET. {W35}GO. {W50}OF ME!
Even as the rain of kicks connects with my face,\n
I wonder:
Does she not understand what she's doing?
The fact that I'm still hanging on by the cloth\n
means every kick is applying more pressure to\n
it...
In that moment, she puts all her strength into\n
a forceful kick, and the cloth rends from end\n
to end.
...Bwuh!?
...welp.
Huh!?
Haku & ???
AAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHH!!??
C-Cold--cold cold cold--!!
Mercifully, the bottom of the pit is filled with\n
water deep enough that I didn't get hurt, but...
Now I'm out in the cold Yamato night, soaked to\n
the bone. In sewage water.
At least it isn't waste water, but that's a\n
small blessing at best.
What did I do to deserve this...?
So, the person we're supposed to be watching out\n
for is gone, you said?
Y-Yeah. B-B-By the time I s-surfaced, she was\n
g-g-g-gone... So c-cold...
I see... I doubt she drowned down there, so\n
it's probably best to assume she fled.
Which... means we accomplished our goal for\n
the night, I think?
Th-Then--ACHOO!!--let's get the hell out of\n
here.
Hey, love?
Wh-What?
You stink.
No shit.
I can see everyone wrinkling their noses,\n
maintaining a cautious distance from me.
C-Can I get a little sympathy, here? This is\n
how you appreciate the guy who d-did all the\n
work?
It's, uh... It's not that we don't appreciate\n
you...
"All the work?" All you did was fall into a\n
hole, though...
Unngh!
Color me astounded that you managed to survive\n
unscathed.
Do I LOOK uns-s-scathed? You're d-doing this on\n
purpose, aren't you!?
Damn it. They could give me some praise for\n
doing the actual job for once...
A job well done, anyway. We should head for the\n
inn. You'll catch a cold at this rate.
Ah--ACHOO!!
A-Are you... all right? Here, use...
Rulutieh passes me a white, elegant-looking\n
handkerchief...
I-I appreciate the sentiment, but I'd feel bad\n
ruining this.
Kuon meets my eyes as I hesitate, trying to\n
decide whether to take the handkerchief or not.
Hmm hmm. Hmmmm.
Kuon laughs, her eyes saying, "I guess it can't\n
be helped."
I'm afraid you'll just have to grin and bear it\n
until we get back to the Hakurokaku.
Huh?
Relenting, Kuon undoes the aperyu around her\n
shoulders and wraps it around mine, instead.
Here. You can borrow this for now.
Walk home like that, and you're gonna get sick.
Oh...
A-Are you sure, dear sister? That'll soil\n
your aperyu...
It's fine. I'll wash it later and it'll be good\n
as new.
Right?
Yes.
Finally, some nice, HOT water for a change...
I rush into the baths, only to find an odd-\n
looking guest using them ahead of me.
...What the hell is THAT?
For a moment, I couldn't even recognize it as\n
a person.
All I can make out is a shape in the quivering,\n
person-sized mass of bubbles.
Must be someone washing off...
Gotta be scrubbing up a storm if they're making\n
that many bubbles.
I take a seat next to the towering pile of\n
bubbles and fill my bath pail with hot water.
Ahh, that's good and hot...
I pour the water over myself several times.\n
It feels all the hotter, after the cold night.
As the water washes over me, I take an\n
experimental sniff at one of my arms...
Urgh. I guess just rinsing won't get rid of\n
the stench.
I grab a soap bar and rub it on a towel until\n
it starts to foam, then turn it on my reeking\n
skin.
*Bubble, bubble, bubble*...
This soap sure foams up a lot, though. I guess\n
the inn is high-end enough to buy the good\n
stuff.
I repeatedly wash, scrub, and rinse, taking a\n
whiff of my skin with each pass as the smell\n
fades.
Finally, I can only smell the soap, the stench\n
of sewage banished. But still...
I can't quite tell if it's fully gone, and I\n
decide to wash once more for good measure.
Geez, the guy next to me is still going at it.\n
He's been washing forever.
It's more like furious scrubbing than "washing",\n
really, going by the shaking. Must be a clean\n
freak.
I finish rinsing off with one last bucket over\n
my head, hot water washing the bubbles away...
The pillar of bubbly cleanliness next to me\n
also reaches for a bucket, seemingly finished.
In any case...
Whew...
What an awful d--Huh?
We both let out a relieved sigh at the same\n
time, and then I look beside me, only to find--
Haku & Nosuri
We both shout and jump away at the same time,\n
almost slipping on the wet floor.
Y-You--!?
You're Nos--I-I mean, that person from earlier!\n
What the hell are YOU doing here!?
I could ask you the same, scoundrel! I'm SURE\n
I put an "occupied" sign on the d--
As Nosuri glares at me, her eyes wander,\n
and she pales.
Th-Th-Th-That--
That?
Sh-Sh-Shut up and p-put that thing away!
She shudders, pointing a shaking finger at me.
I follow the line of her finger to--Oh.\n
THAT thing.
...My bad.
She's hardly making an unreasonable demand, so\n
I reach for a towel and wrap myself in it.
...Aren't you gonna cover up too, though?\n
I mean, everything's sort of...
Sort of what?
ACK!
Only now does she seem to realize she's just as\n
naked as I am, and she covers herself with her\n
arms.
Of course, it's difficult to hide your entire\n
body with just your arms, and she squirms\n
awkwardly.
Gah--I understand, now. Y-You shadowed me all\n
the way here, didn't you!?
What?
Don't play the fool! I KNOW you were following\n
me!
That must be why you dropped me into that pit,\n
scoundrel. Forcing me into this vulnerable\n
state...
Oh, come on! Why would I go through that much\n
trouble?
Quit feigning ignorance, coward! How DARE you\n
force me to reveal the location of my secret\n
base?
...Nobody said anything about that. She kinda\n
just blew the secret on her own.
I'm kinda hoping she doesn't just blurt out\n
that she's a thief.
My job's over and done with. I don't want to be\n
any more involved in this mess than I already\n
am...
And it's only gonna get worse unless I put a\n
stop to it now.
Ahem--You're sounding awfully suspicious, you\n
know. Almost as if you've been up to no good.
Like planning on a heist on a certain mansion,\n
for example.
Hyeh!?
Good, she's realizing her mistake. Hopefully she\n
won't make any more careless remarks after--
How did you know!?
You've gotta be kidding me. You aren't denying\n
it!?
I gave you a chance to get out of it, and you\n
confess anyway!?
Wha--!? O-Oh, no, a leading question!?
To fall for such a fundamental trick--\n
how could I be so careless?
I don't think I can talk sense with this girl.\n
What the hell am I supposed to do, here?
Hah...
Ha haaah ha...
AHAHAHAHAHA!! It seems you've found me out.\n
So be it!
Your suspicions are indeed correct. I am the\n
one and only Nosuri, of the Nosuri Thieves!
Despite struggling to preserve her modesty just\n
now, she suddenly stands up proudly, arms\n
folded.
I am but a vagrant, a mendicant of fortune who\n
seeks to restore her tarnished family name!
But above these, I am a champion of the people--\n
the poor of Yamato who suffer from its\n
corruption!
Uh... OK.
I pretty much know all of this already, so I'm\n
not... really sure how to respond?
And wasn't she JUST trying to cover herself?\n
I can... kinda see everything at this point.
What's the matter? I introduced myself.\n
Now it's your turn.
What, I have to do one too?
Somehow, I get the feeling telling her my name\n
is just asking for trouble. Oh, well...
I'm Haku.
I see. Haku, is it...? Where have I heard\n
that name before?
Wait, you've heard of me?
I... can't seem to remember.\n
Therefore, it must be my imagination! Yes.
Get my hopes up just to crush them, why don't\n
you? Yeesh...
But I'll admit you're very good. You're the\n
first person to corner me so completely.
I understand you're praising me, but somehow\n
that doesn't make me feel any better.
So, what now? Do you plan on capturing me and\n
handing me over to the capital guards?
I just told you I'm not planning anything!
Give it a rest, already! I've been pulled deep\n
enough into all this as it is.
Then that means... aha. I see.
I never expected... someone in this place who\n
shares my ideals...
At last, my hard work is starting to pay off.
And now she's just muttering to herself.
You! How would you like to become one of my\n
bandits?
You're quite skilled, to have cornered me like\n
this. I would be glad to have you as an ally.
What? There was nothing "skilled" about it.\n
You sort of stumbled into this all on your own.
What do you say? Not a bad proposition, isn't\n
it?
Crap... She seems pretty serious about this.
I'm afraid I have to modestly decline.
Ahahaha! There's no need for modesty in front\n
of me.
...Did you hear the part where I said "decline"?
She keeps going on about people in need of\n
saving from oppression, but are there really\n
that many?
Yamato is a peaceful, prosperous nation, and\n
the people always seem lively to me. Not much\n
suffering.
Hey, can I ask you something? What about the\n
capital guards? Can't you leave peacekeeping\n
to them?
From what I've heard, Uk--Oshtor seems to be\n
doing a good job of keeping the place safe.
Hm... that Imperial Guard man, eh...?
He just won't do, I'm afraid. I'll admit he's\n
done good things, but he deliberates and delays\n
too much.
Those who reach out for help are those who need\n
it immediately, and he hesitates in favor of\n
planning.
By the time he has his schemes ready to act\n
upon, it's often far too late.
Whenever we follow through with our plans, he\n
always comes to the scene in a rush, taken off-\n
guard...
Like a carrion bird arriving late to pick our\n
prey clean. So much for the Twin Shields of\n
Yamato.
Oh, right. I almost forgot she isn't aware of\n
her group's alliance with Oshtor.
I was wondering why she hasn't been made aware,\n
but... I think I understand now.
Oshtor wouldn't want to work with an impulsive\n
person. She'd do things her way and ruin his\n
plans.
Do you understand, now? The hero of the people\n
isn't Oshtor, but me, Nosuri the noble thief!
She proudly puffs out her chest, which\n
promptly jiggles.
...She seems to have gotten so impassioned in\n
her speech that she forgot what kind of state\n
she's in.
Ah, but I suppose it's unfair to push this kind\n
of decision on you so quickly.
That's... not the issue here.
When we meet again, I'll be wanting your\n
answer!
Smiling with smug satisfaction, Nosuri turns\n
for the exit, still stark naked.
Ah, Sir Haku... I brought you a clean change\n
of c-clo--
*Fwump*...
I peer around the corner to find Rulutieh, a\n
rumpled pile of clothes at her feet, staring\n
at Nosuri...
We shall meet again. I expect you'll have a\n
more favorable answer for me, next time.
Sir... Haku...?
I swear I can see the life draining from\n
Rulutieh's eyes as she stares vacantly ahead.
Pardon me.
Hm? Oh, hullo.
One afternoon, Munechika suddenly came by our\n
headquarters...
I remember you. You were here a little while\n
back, weren't you? Miss, erm...
Lady Munechika.
Right! The general who takes care of the\n
princess, that's you.
I'm Atuy! Pleasure to meet you.
Atuy?
Pleasant surprise spreads across Munechika's\n
face upon hearing the name.
Lord Soyankekur's daughter, correct?
Oh, you know about me?
I owe a great deal to milord Soyankekur.\n
He taught me much when I was merely a novice.
Oh, he--no, no. None of that, please.
Hm?
This inn is a hideout, yeah? For those of us who\n
want to shed all the politics and the "milords."
Ah, I see. My deepest apologies.
Would you quit making stuff up about this\n
place, Atuy?
How can we help you? If you're looking for the\n
princess, I'm afraid she's not here. For once.
Not to worry. I'm quite certain Her Highness\n
is absorbed in her studies, at present.
We're... talking about the same princess, here,\n
right?
The princess's frequent escapes finally roused\n
the high priestess into giving her quite the\n
scolding.
It takes a fair lot to try her patience.\n
Trust me, we'll have no royal problems for\n
some time.
I should think that even if Her Highness makes\n
an attempt, she'll find her guards doubled\n
today.
Well, that's a shame.
Nah. Serves her right. Retribution for the\n
wicked.
I didn't think anyone existed who could give her\n
a scolding and actually be listened to, though.
The high priestess is the handler of all\n
spiritual affairs within the Mikado's court.
In addition to those duties, she serves as\n
Her Highness's tutor.
Tutor? I thought you were in charge of that.
It would be disrespectful to claim the full\n
title for myself, truly.
I merely take over certain duties when my\n
responsibilities as a General do not consume\n
my time.
I see. And you're here because...? Shouldn't you\n
be near the princess if you're her bodyguard?
Munechika glances at the ceiling for a moment.
Ahem! I-It's quite... rare to see Her Highness\n
working diligently. My presence would only\n
distract.
More importantly, as Yamato's future ruler,\n
it is paramount that she understand its people\n
fully.
Equally paramount, however, is the princess'\n
safety. I have come to assess the security of\n
this inn.
I kinda see where you're coming from, but...\n
maybe we should call the princess here first?
Th-This is a matter of utmost secrecy!\n
N-National security! To speak of it to the\n
princess, you--
"Utmost secrecy," my ass. You're digging your\n
own grave, here.
Oh, come on, Haku. Give her a break.
Kuon?
She came all this way to pay us a visit.\n
Why don't you let her in?
I thank you for your kindness.
Here, have a seat.
Thank you.
Trailing behind Kuon, Munechika pauses as she\n
catches sight of something...
Hm...? What is this?
Her eyes fall on the plate Atuy is holding.
Oh, this? Just some Rulu. It's getting real\n
popular around the capital.
"Rulu"? Ah, the sweet I've been hearing so much\n
about. It almost looks like a fruit from the\n
south.
That's all you've been having for snacks,\n
lately, Atuy... Taken a liking to them, huh?
Hee! They're my new favorite. They sort of have\n
a texture like Kurarin, don't they?
*Jiggle*...
...I wonder what the texture would be like if\n
I ate Kurarin, though...
*Jiggle-jiggle-jiggle-jiggle--*
Oh, come on, now he's gone all pale.
Anyway, would you like one, Munechika?
May I?
Atuy holds a piece of Rulu on a toothpick for\n
Munechika, who hesitates slightly...
Then, with Atuy smiling innocently at her, she\n
nods and takes a bite.
How do you like it?
Hrm... This is...
Quite good! Its texture... somehow soft, springy,\n
and slippery at once. I've never tasted its like.
But this is... hm. This won't do.
I see, now... How could Her Highness concentrate\n
on studying with temptations like this about?
One must know one's enemies, after all. I shall\n
make this sacrifice for the well-being of the\n
princess.
*Hromf, munch--*
Oh... Oh, my...
In a matter of seconds, Atuy's entire plate\n
disappears.
Decadent indeed. Thank you for the Rulu,\n
Lady Atuy.
Munechika clasps her hands together as a sign\n
of thanks.
My Rulu...
Ah, was I... not supposed to eat it all?
D-Don't... don't worry about it. There's plenty\n
more where that came from.
Oh... Hello, Miss Munechika...
Rulutieh appears in the doorway.
Rulie! Perfect timing. Could you bring some\n
more Rulu?
Did, uhm... Did you eat all of it already?
Not... exactly.
You didn't...?
"I" didn't, no. But could you make some more?
Uhm... I'm sorry, but... I don't have any more\n
ingredients for it...
Hey, it's all right. We'll just pick up a lot\n
more tree sap next time, OK?
Ah, about that. Due to Rulu's wild popularity,\n
sap extraction has seen an unprecedented spike.
Small forests around the capital have begun to\n
suffer severely, so extraction is now\n
restricted.
...I guess that's what happens when it's people\n
who are attracted to it, instead of just ants.
Indeed. It has me thinking...
I-If you'd still like sweets of some kind,\n
you can... try these...
Rulutieh offers a different plate to the\n
dejected Atuy.
On it sits a batch of confections, crisp, golden\n
and round.
What's this?
I was working on a new recipe today...
Oh, hey, it looks like they turned out pretty\n
well this time.
Y-Yes... just as you said, Sir Haku, the key\n
was to fine-tune the temperature of the fire...
I seem to remember seeing you two sneak off to\n
the kitchens earlier. Working on something new?
Thinking up a new kind of sweet, love?
I guess. We just tried baking some thinned amam\n
dough with kondens filling in a gap in the\n
middle.
The dough puffs up when we go to bake it, so\n
we're calling them... Well, "Puffs."
Puffs?
That's a strange name, but... it fits, I guess.
Indeed. An exotic name and appearance alike...
Well, we've gotten this far, but we still have\n
to taste-test. So...
Y-Yes... I'd appreciate it if you could all try\n
some and tell me what you think...
Hee hee. Don't mind if I do.
I will participate as well.
Me too.
...I am also interested in sampling them for\n
myself.
Each of us grabs a puff from the platter.\n
In unison, we each take an experimental bite...
This is--!
Reeaally goooood~!
Mm... The crunchy exterior of the amam mixing\n
with the kondens' rich flavor...
A-Ah, I'm glad you all like it...
Astounding. The puffed dough creates a hollow\n
in the middle for the kondens... but how?
Y-You just have to knead properly. My first few\n
batches all shriveled up before we discovered\n
that...
Kneading? True, that process softens the dough\n
and eliminates bubbles, but puffing it up...?
Well...
Rulutieh looks over to me, a little confused\n
on how to answer the question.
She made it, sure, but I was the one who\n
instructed her on how to do it, since it's...\n
complicated.
It's mostly because of the moisture trapped in\n
the dough.
Moisture? Wait, were you the one who came up\n
with the idea for this, Haku?
W-Well... yeah. I understand the principles\n
behind it, but I just don't have the practical\n
skills.
If you wouldn't mind, I am curious as to the\n
mechanics of how you made the dough rise that\n
way.
Well, it took a lot of trial and error, but we\n
found if we make the dough into a--
As I begin to explain, I realize I have an\n
opportunity to have a little fun.
What's this? Could it be the great court scholar\n
Nekone has finally found a problem she can't\n
crack?
Nngh...
V-Very well. If there are no other ingredients,\n
I should be able to discern the specifics on my\n
own...
She pops a puff into her mouth as she makes\n
this proclamation, focusing intently.
...Amam powder... milk, salt... eggs...\n
but that's not all...?
The kondens is making it hard to concentrate...
She seems to be too distracted by the taste to\n
analyze it properly.
If you want to analyze just the outer shell,\n
we can prepare one on its own for you?
N-No! I mean--it would be a waste.
To think such flavor could exist in this\n
world... My experiences are more limited than\n
I thought.
Munechika continues to eat one puff after\n
another, showing no signs of stopping any time\n
soon.
Mmm. I think it might be even BETTER than the\n
Rulu...
Please, have more. I made plenty...
Really? Aw, my little Rulie!\n
Love ya, girl.
You can cook just about anything, can't you?\n
I dunno what I did to deserve you.
You're just too good to be true.
Can I just marry you, Rulie?
H-Huh--? M-M-Marr--
I-I--I, uhm--
I don't think she's serious, Rulutieh.
Huh...?
Only joking, Rulie. I do love you, really,\n
just... not like that.
H-Heh... Y-You took me by surprise, there...
Please, Miss Atuy, have as many puffs as you\n
like...
Hee hee. Yes, ma'am.
Atuy reaches for the plate...
...only to find that the mountain of sweets\n
has been reduced to a single, lonesome puff.
In that moment, another hand snaps out like a\n
coiled snake and snatches the final puff up.
Huh?
Mmf... Such sugary bliss...
Positively delectable. I must thank you for\n
your hospitality.
...ah, perhaps I've eaten more than my share.\n
But I feel strangely wonderful!
With that, Munechika finds a couch to sink into,\n
visibly relaxed.
Atuy continues staring absentmindedly at the\n
empty plate.
I... suppose I feel a little bad for her.
